description: Information Safety Experiment — Network Data Encryption, Authentication, and Transmission

features.name: Sensor Data Simulation & Collection
features.description: Simulate sensor data (temperature, humidity, pressure) as JSON strings for network transmission.
done_criteria: A function generates structured sensor data as a JSON string.
Data includes timestamp, sensor_id, and multiple readings.
Test script validates data format.
evidence: data/sensor_data.py lines 25-48 (generate_reading) — includes timestamp, sensor_id, readings

features.name: Encryption Algorithm (AES)
features.description: Implement AES encryption from scratch (no external crypto libraries). Support both encryption and decryption.
done_criteria: AES implementation includes S-Box, key expansion, SubBytes, ShiftRows, MixColumns, AddRoundKey.
Both encrypt and decrypt functions work correctly.
Test script encrypts a plaintext and decrypts it back to the original.
evidence: crypto/aes_crypto.py — S-Box (lines 25-73), key_expansion (lines 273-319), sub_bytes (149-158), shift_rows (171-189), mix_columns (209-236), add_round_key (258-266)
test_aes.py — 23 tests all passing, including NIST FIPS-197 test vector

features.name: RSA Key Generation & Key Encryption
features.description: Implement RSA key generation, and use RSA to encrypt the AES session key for secure key exchange.
done_criteria: RSA key pair (public/private) is generated programmatically.
RSA encrypts a session key; RSA decrypts it back to the original.
Test script validates key encryption/decryption round-trip.

features.name: Message Authentication (HMAC-SHA256)
features.description: Implement HMAC using SHA-256 from scratch. Attach authentication tag to encrypted data.
done_criteria: SHA-256 and HMAC implemented without external libraries.
Authenticator is appended to the ciphertext before transmission.
Test script verifies a valid tag and rejects a tampered message.
evidence: test_hmac.py — 20 tests all passing (SHA-256 known vectors, HMAC key/message variations, tamper detection)

features.name: Network Transmission — Server (Receiver)
features.description: Build a TCP server that receives encrypted+authenticated data, verifies the HMAC tag, decrypts, and outputs original sensor data.
done_criteria: TCP server listens and accepts connections.
Receives a frame containing [encrypted_payload + hmac_tag].
Verifies HMAC, decrypts AES, prints original sensor data.
Test script runs server + client and validates end-to-end.
evidence: test_server.py — 4 tests all passing (roundtrip, tamper rejection, invalid HMAC, 10-readings batch)

features.name: Network Transmission — Client (Sender)
features.description: Build a TCP client that generates sensor data, encrypts with AES, encrypts AES key with RSA, computes HMAC, and sends.
done_criteria: Client connects to server, sends complete encrypted+authenticated frame.
End-to-end test: client sends data, server receives and decrypts correctly.
Tamper test: modify the payload mid-transmission and confirm server rejects it.
evidence: network/client.py lines 93-123 (build_frame) — encrypt AES + RSA encrypt key + pack_frame
network/client.py lines 126-158 (send_frame) — TCP connect, sendall, receive response
test_client.py — 9 tests all passing (frame format, HMAC validity, size checks, deterministic key, roundtrip, tamper rejection, invalid HMAC, multi-batch)

features.name: Integration & Report
features.description: Integrate all components into a single runnable project. Ensure end-to-end flow works. Prepare experiment report structure.
done_criteria: Single `python main.py` runs the full pipeline.
All test scripts pass.
Report sections match experiment requirements.
evidence: main.py lines 122-204 (run_local_e2e) — 6-phase pipeline: keygen → sensor data → AES → RSA → HMAC → TCP E2E
main.py lines 263-306 (main) — argparse with --demo / --e2e / default mode
All 6 test scripts pass: 15+23+28+20+9+4 = 99 tests total
Standalone demo and E2E pipeline both verified with real output

features.name: Web Frontend
features.description: Browser-based web UI for the full pipeline: pipeline visualizer, crypto playground, E2E one-click and manual modes. Zero external dependencies — stdlib http.server + vanilla JS.
done_criteria: Web UI accessible at http://localhost:8080
Four tabs: Pipeline Wizard, Crypto Playground, E2E Auto, E2E Manual
All 9 API endpoints functional
18 HTTP API tests passing
Packet capture: server records raw TCP bytes, saves to captures/*.hex
File save: sensor data saved to captures/*.json on each received frame
Frontend displays decrypted sensor data and captured packet hex
Zero external dependencies — stdlib only
evidence: server_api.py — HTTP server + 9 API endpoints + static file serving
test_server_api.py — 18 tests (sensor-data, aes-enc, aes-dec, keys, encrypt-key, hmac, verify-hmac, static files, error handling)
network/server.py — receive_frame now returns raw_frame, handle_client returns structured dict, run_server saves captures/*.hex and sensor_*.json
server_api.py — _handle_e2e_connection records capture_hex and sensor_data, e2e-full response includes decrypted data
